# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
 name: nodejsappdeploymemt  # unique identifier for this deployment (must be alphanumeric, no special characters or spaces) and it should match the k8s api server' naming convention. can begin with a lower case letter to avoid collision namespaces conflict while setting up your cluster due strict rules in Kubernetes
spec:
 replicas: 3   # number of pod instances that will be running at one time (default value is taken by Pod Status)    it indicates how many units you want, can either auto scale or fixed. Auto-scaling groups have a scaling policy to change the replica set size according actual metrics in terms as CPU utilization for instance
 selector:  # used while creating pods which has same label selectors (app and team must match with k8' s api server)   It enables you filter Pod instances that are going into your Kubernetes cluster. In this case, it should be set to nodejs-team or any unique name for the purpose of identifying these replicas on different nodes in a given namespace
 template:  # spec details about pods which will run inside 'selector'   It includes how containers like NodeJS application needs environment variables and volumes attached. Kubernetes Pod manages container instances by creating new ones, deleting old ones when needed (default behavior), until the limit is reached if any specific limits are set for each sidecars of our microservice
